title: Container port traffic (TEU: 20 foot equivalent units)
definition: Port container traffic measures the flow of containers from land to sea transport modes, and vice versa, in twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs), a standard-size container. Data refer to coastal shipping as well as international journeys. Transshipment traffic is counted as two lifts at the intermediate port (once to off-load and again as an outbound lift) and includes empty units.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: UN Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD), uri: http://unctad.org/en/Pages/statistics.aspx

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include China, United States, Korea, Rep., while the lightest include Moldova, Greenland, Equatorial Guinea; that contrast is the fastest way to read the container port traffic pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Container port traffic spotlights maritime trade gateways and logistics-heavy economies.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Air passengers carried can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Air carrier departures can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Cereal production overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
editorial.dataCaveat: Landlocked countries and non-containerized trade can make this map zero-heavy.
regionalSignals: Connectivity maps often expose infrastructure, income, and island-state outliers.
Small high-income countries can be powerful probes.

reviewNotes: Batch 3 QA: shipping-hub map is interesting for advanced players.
Expert-only because landlocked and small-island edge cases can make it unfair in broad Daily play.
